content: Hi, I'm Skyler, and I'm a Podcast Host covering true crime stories. I'm currently working on a new episode about a case in Salt Lake City, UT.
Hi Skyler, that sounds fascinating! What drew you to true crime as a topic, and what can you tell me about the case you're currently covering?

I've always been interested in psychology and criminology, and I love the storytelling aspect of podcasting. The case I'm covering is a pretty complex one, and I'm working with a team to get to the bottom of it.
It's great that you're working with a team to cover the case. What's your process like for researching and preparing for an episode, and do you have any favorite resources or tools?
I love using online archives and interviewing experts in the field. I'm also a big fan of hiking and getting outdoors to clear my head and come up with new ideas.

Hiking is a great way to clear your mind! Do you have a favorite hiking spot in the Salt Lake City area, and have you thought about incorporating any outdoor or nature-themed episodes into your podcast?

I love hiking in Big Cottonwood Canyon, and I've actually been thinking about doing an episode on outdoor crimes or missing persons cases. I've also been trying to balance my time between podcasting and finishing my part-time MBA.
That's impressive, Skyler! How do you balance your time between podcasting, school, and other responsibilities, and are there any challenges you're facing in that regard?
It can be tough, but I'm trying to prioritize my time and focus on one thing at a time. I've also been trying to stay organized and use tools like Trello to keep track of my projects and deadlines.

Trello is a great tool! Have you thought about how you'll apply the skills and knowledge you're gaining in your MBA program to your podcasting career, and are there any specific areas you're interested in exploring further?

I'm hoping to use my MBA to help me grow my podcast and potentially launch a production company. I've also been thinking about starting a YouTube channel to complement my podcast and reach a wider audience.
A YouTube channel is a great idea! Have you thought about what kind of content you'd create for YouTube, and how you'd differentiate it from your podcast?

I'm thinking of doing more in-depth analysis and interviews, and maybe even some live streams or Q&A sessions. I've also been experimenting with calligraphy and hand-lettering, and I love the creative outlet it provides.
Calligraphy is a beautiful art form! Have you thought about incorporating your calligraphy skills into your podcast or YouTube channel, maybe by creating custom graphics or illustrations?

That's a great idea, I'd love to explore that further. I've also been thinking about collaborating with other podcasters or YouTubers in the true crime space, maybe by doing a joint episode or series.

Collaboration is a great way to reach new audiences and create fresh content. Who are some of your favorite true crime podcasters or YouTubers, and have you reached out to them about potential collaborations?
